images.alt:
Sure. How much do you think you should pay me to use my name?
It's really important to think about attribution and think about impersonation, and so on. As an expert, you have a trade you make on the internet. The idea is that when you put content out there, myself included, you hope people use it. You want to refer to other people's content.
You want people to link to you. You really, really hope they attribute you when they do. When somebody uses your content, should they attribute you? Of course. And to attribute you, you have to use your name.
There's a different line which is, should people be able to impersonate you? And I think that is a very different standard. And we saw the lawsuit. Respectfully, we believe the claims are without merit. The idea that the feature is impersonation is quite a big stretch. Every mention was very clearly, "This is inspired not only by this person, but also inspired by a specific work from this specific person, with a clear attributed link to get back to them." It's far from that test lof impersonation].

@jaredwhite I've been trying to figure this out for a while, too.

My understanding is that you can host your own PDS, which is the authoritative source for your identity and your own content. The Bluesky relay scrapes your PDS (somehow?), and Bluesky users see your posts that way.

What's less clear to me is how you would read posts from Bluesky users if you're not using their relay and/or app views. I think that part is still centralized or you have to run your own relay, which is expensive.

»Demons' Crest« entsteht bei Production I.G (Kaiju No. 8) unter der Regie von Kenichirō Komaya (Nina the Starry Bride), Shinji Ushiro (A Condition Called Love) fungiert als Chief Director, die Series Composition übernimmt Eiji Umehara (Suicide Squad ISEKAI), das Character Design stammt von Yukiko Horiguchi (Tamako Market) und die Musik komponieren Yūki Hayashi (My Hero Academia) und Naoyuki Chikatani.

📜 Beschreibung 》
In dem neuen VRMMORPG „Actual Magic“ werden plötzlich ganze Schulklassen gefangen gehalten und aus einem Spiel wird bitterer Ernst. Der elfjährige Yuma Ashihara kämpft mit seinen Mitschülern ums Überleben, während Monster, riesige Bestien und unerklärliche Regeln ihre Welt bestimmen. Als eine Klassenkameradin zu einem Ungeheuer wird und ihr Freund Nagi verschwindet, bleibt ihnen nur eine unheimliche Hilfe: der Dämon Valac, der Yumas Zwillingsschwester Sawa Ashihara besessen hat. Unter Valacs Anleitung startet die Gruppe eine gefährliche Rettungsmission, doch auf sie wartet mehr, als sie erwartet haben.

images.alt:
📜 Beschreibung 》
Plötzlich erscheinen auf der ganzen Welt geheimnisvolle Gräber, in denen Relikte verborgen liegen, die ihren Besitzern besondere Kräfte verleihen. Jooheon Suh lebt davon, diese Gräber aufzuspüren, auszuheben und ihre Schätze an sich zu reißen. Doch ausgerechnet sein eigener Arbeitgeber verrät ihn, und Jooheon steht kurz davor, durch ein mächtiges Relikt zu sterben. In diesem Moment geschieht das Unmögliche: Er wacht 15 Jahre in der Vergangenheit wieder auf, in einer Zeit, bevor die ersten Gräber und Relikte überhaupt aufgetaucht sind. Mit diesem Wissensvorsprung erhält Jooheon eine zweite Chance. Angetrieben von Rache will er diesmal niemandem mehr vertrauen und die Ereignisse zu seinem eigenen Vorteil lenken. Schritt für Schritt beginnt er, sich die wertvollsten Relikte zuerst zu sichern und seinen Aufstieg vorzubereiten. So startet eine Geschichte über Macht, Verrat und den Versuch, mit Wissen aus der Zukunft selbst zum Tomb Raider King zu werden.

external.title: Original post on blorbo.social

record.bridgyOriginalText:
So we know that there's a trend in both professionally published work and fanfiction of... Conflict Sanitization? I.e your serial killer oc only kills "bad people", your historical characters have no homophobia or racism, everyone uses therapy speak to talk out their problems.

And we know the issue is that a lot of authors are afraid of being called out on social media. People are afraid of writing conflict because they are afraid of their audience. Their audience will say that they endorse everything that their characters say and do.

Is the solution to make fun of these authors who are afraid? Are there real consequences for being dogpiled on social media? Has anyone participating in the conversation been dogpiled, and having every post on the first page of search results being "this person is a bad person for writing xyz, don't support them unless YOU ARE also a bad person"?

I think a lot of times, a bad review or two doesn't affect one's livelihood. But full pages of constant attacks... Hmmm... It can and will affect financial income, especially when these people campaign to call your workplace and make you lose your job or like destroy your work when they see you irl (these things have happened). And it will affect mental health having a constant flood of messages saying "kys".

I personally prefer to just post my unfiltered art of "difficult subjects" and I guess accept if I get a flood of terrible comments. I have a bit of disdain for people who engage in targeted hate campaigns, and especially for people who engage in hate campaigns publically but admit they enjoy "difficult subjects" on their side account.

What can be done though? If authors don't want to write "difficult subjects" because they are afraid of their audience, is it proper to blame the author for not wanting to get their financials or mental health negatively affected? Is playing it safe to scrape by and afford groceried a bad thing? I feel like people apply this a lot to authors and artists, they have a moral imperative to do... Something. But if you apply it to any other job its like, are you gonna yell at the dude who just works a regular retail job instead of chasing his dream of being a singer with no health insurance or guranteed income in a world of genai?

Is there anything we can do about the anonymous swarming "audience" that inflames moral OCD? Are people talking to their own peers and shaming them for participating in dogpiling? Or is it easier to talk down about the far-away almost imaginary Author, rather than talking to one's oomfie who is making rude qrts and writing 100 page inflammatory google docs? I feel like people are very afraid to confront their own friends about their poor behavior. A lot comes down to "i do not want to lose my social and support network, i want to fit in" on every side. So ppl will just say "art sucks now"...

Do these idiots not realize this is going to inevitably blow up in their faces? Absolute clowns.

record.text:
Mass-produced AI content is like planting plastic trees in a forest.

It looks like growth from a distance but provides no oxygen, no life.

The ecosystem always rejects the artificial.

AI Slop: Digital pollution.

Human Craft: The Grind.

One poisons the soil of the ecosystem. The other cultivates it. YouTube is finally weeding its garden.

They told you AI would make you a creator.

They were wrong.

It turned you into a factory worker, churning out digital noise. True creation requires a soul.
Blocking all generative AI, I don't care.
Nobody remembers Oliver Miller. Ok, we do.
Seriously, Red Hat Guy looks like one of my relatives.
